name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Quick request: Will you sign our card to thank Virginia's teachers?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
As the next Governor of Virginia, I’m going to make sure our teachers have the support they deserve — because every dollar we invest in them is an investment in generations to come.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107212520/543956833/-1469667980?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWVmM2EwM2ItYzAyYS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=MsW_akU2Vc9GsqyO3TmYvIpmIHINyGwssHGVeHK6rkQ=&emci=17f3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=1ef3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=8470899)
---
| Peter, as the mom of three girls in Virginia's public schools, a graduate of them myself, and the daughter-in-law of a lifelong Virginia educator, I know firsthand the transformative power of teachers.

Today, on Teacher Appreciation Day, I'm going to tell you about a teacher who is near and dear to my heart. But first, I have a quick request:

[_**Will you sign our card to thank Virginia's teachers and tell me about a teacher who changed your life?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107212521/543956834/-1469667980?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWVmM2EwM2ItYzAyYS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=MsW_akU2Vc9GsqyO3TmYvIpmIHINyGwssHGVeHK6rkQ=&emci=17f3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=1ef3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=8470899)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107212522/543956835/-1469667980?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWVmM2EwM2ItYzAyYS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=MsW_akU2Vc9GsqyO3TmYvIpmIHINyGwssHGVeHK6rkQ=&emci=17f3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=1ef3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=8470899)
Señora Green was my ninth grade Spanish and world studies teacher.

Looking back, she didn't just teach me how to conjugate complicated verbs. She taught me to love languages. She built up my confidence. She believed in me, and in turn, I believed in myself.

_That's what great teachers do: they shape the next generation._

Now, if you haven't already, won't you take this small step to show Virginia's over 100,000 educators just how much they mean to us?

[_**Sign our card to thank Virginia’s teachers and, if you’re willing, share a story about a teacher who made a difference in your life!**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107212523/543956836/-1469667980?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWVmM2EwM2ItYzAyYS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=MsW_akU2Vc9GsqyO3TmYvIpmIHINyGwssHGVeHK6rkQ=&emci=17f3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=1ef3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=8470899)
| [SIGN THE CARD ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107212524/543956837/-1469667980?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWVmM2EwM2ItYzAyYS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=MsW_akU2Vc9GsqyO3TmYvIpmIHINyGwssHGVeHK6rkQ=&emci=17f3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=1ef3a03b-c02a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=8470899)
---
As the next Governor of Virginia, I’m going to make sure our teachers have the support they deserve — because every dollar we invest in _them_ is an investment in _generations_ to come.

Thank you for showing your support,

Abigail Spanberger

Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene **[went off at Democrat Dan Goldman](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e0c00b-62fa-430b-a55b-159a8f378c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after he scolded Republicans for deporting illegal gang members and terrorists without being "determined to be a member of a foreign gang by someone other than the President."
**[She did not hold back...](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e0c00b-62fa-430b-a55b-159a8f378c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e0c00b-62fa-430b-a55b-159a8f378c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e0c00b-62fa-430b-a55b-159a8f378c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I think my colleague across the aisle is very confused and very uniformed on what a Special Interest Alien is," Greene said before explaining that an SIA is "a non-U.S. person who, based on an analysis of travel patterns, potentially poses a national security risk to the United States or its interests. SIAs are people who are from countries,... that promote or protect terrorism."
Greene criticized Democrats for "opposing" the GOP publishing the number of SIAs identified and detained in the United States, "It's just the number, we're just telling the American people how many, that's it!"
"And just to go further..." **[Greene said, getting ready to really tear them a new one,](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e0c00b-62fa-430b-a55b-159a8f378c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** "On our side of the aisle we trust the American people and we believe they deserve transparency, they deserve the truth,... the American people are the taxpayers who pay our paychecks! They pay for this building! They fund this government even though the government has plunged them into $36 trillion in debt, and opened up the borders for the last 4 years, and allowed it to be flooded with illegal aliens, and the Biden administration gave them social security numbers!!"
"This should be simple!" Greene exploded. "We are Americans, we serve THIS country, not foreign countries!"
Watch the congresswoman unload on every Democrat in the room for selling out the American people:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e0c00b-62fa-430b-a55b-159a8f378c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# **Laura Ingraham debates young Democrat on doomed future of his party**
Laura Ingraham **[gave Democrat Rep. Jake Auchincloss a major reality check](https://substack.com/redirect/1254b02b-8a57-41db-a480-7b190dd75d60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** live on Fox News about the dark future of the Democrat Party, post-Biden.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/1254b02b-8a57-41db-a480-7b190dd75d60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/1254b02b-8a57-41db-a480-7b190dd75d60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Ingraham asked Auchincloss about Kamala Harris' speech last week in which she condemned President Donald Trump's first 100 days in office.
**["Do you think the Democrats are answering the concerns of the middle class](https://substack.com/redirect/1254b02b-8a57-41db-a480-7b190dd75d60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that have began to turn away from the Democrat standard bearers?" Ingraham asked the congressman.
"Democrats have an opportunity to answer those concerns," Auchincloss answered. "Democrats have a chance to say 'we are now a party of free enterprise, free trade, with free people to build a strong middle class,... here's how we're gonna do it...'"
"So why didn't you do it?" Ingraham fired back. " Why didn't Biden do that? You had 4 years of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris and for part of that time you had a Democrat congress. You had all that time, Biden said it was going to be a new era for America, so what happened?!"
**[Watch Ingraham put the Democrat on the spot](https://substack.com/redirect/1254b02b-8a57-41db-a480-7b190dd75d60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in front of millions watching:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/6060aa11-6e73-43c9-a515-bfa513486d76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# **The American working class is done getting screwed...**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/640b865b-3ca0-4bdb-91cb-f1cfd253f0e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**By Matt Miller**
One hundred days into President Donald J. Trump's second term, and the man is doing exactly what he said he'd do. No excuses--just results. The American people, who grind every day and sacrifice to give their families a better life, are finally seeing a White House that works for them, not the DC elites, not China or the the globalist marxists. Finally America has a President for the working class, and it's about damn time.
For decades, we've been sold a lie: globalism is good, cheap Amazon crap from sweatshops is progress, and America's job is to keep up with the latest Apple iphone while our factories rust and our workers get canned. That's not an economy; that's a scam. Trump's tariffs are the economic reality check we've needed for a long time. It's not about punishing other countries; it's about giving America what it needs. His tariffs are forcing companies to think twice before shipping jobs overseas. They're bringing manufacturing back to places like Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Michigan--places the elites forgot about long ago.
[Read Full Piece Here](https://substack.com/redirect/640b865b-3ca0-4bdb-91cb-f1cfd253f0e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Marjorie Taylor Greene after he scolded Republicans for deporting illegal gang members and terrorists without being "determined to be a member of a foreign gang by someone other than the President." "I think my colleague across the aisle is very confused and very uniformed on what a Special Interest Alien is," Greene said before explaining that an SIA is "a non-U.S. person who, based on an analysis of travel patterns, potentially poses a national security risk to the United States or its interests. SIAs are people who are from countries,... that promote or protect terrorism." Greene criticized Democrats for "opposing" the GOP publishing the number of SIAs identified and detained in the United States, "It's just the number, we're just telling the American people how many, that's it!" "And just to go further..." "On our side of the aisle we trust the American people and we believe they deserve transparency, they deserve the truth,... the American people are the taxpayers who pay our paychecks! They pay for this building! They fund this government even though the government has plunged them into $36 trillion in debt, and opened up the borders for the last 4 years, and allowed it to be flooded with illegal aliens, and the Biden administration gave them social security numbers!!" "This should be simple!" Greene exploded. "We are Americans, we serve THIS country, not foreign countries!" Watch the congresswoman unload on every Democrat in the room for selling out the American people: Apple 's Starlink Update Sparks Huge Earning Opportunity | | ---|---|--- Apple just secretly added Starlink satellite support to iPhones through iOS 18.3. One of the biggest potential winners? Mode's EarnPhone already reaches +45M users that have earned over $325M, and that's before global satellite coverage. With SpaceX eliminating "dead zones" worldwide, Mode's earning technology can now reach billions more in unbanked and rural populations worldwide. Their global expansion is perfectly timed, and you still have a chance to Mode's recent 32,481% revenue growth and their newly reserved Nasdaq ticker $MODE puts them one step closer to a potential IPO. Laura Ingraham debates young Democrat on doomed future of his party Laura Ingraham live on Fox News about the dark future of the Democrat Party, post-Biden. Ingraham asked Auchincloss about Kamala Harris' speech last week in which she condemned President Donald Trump's first 100 days in office. that have began to turn away from the Democrat standard bearers?" Ingraham asked the congressman. "Democrats have an opportunity to answer those concerns," Auchincloss answered. "Democrats have a chance to say 'we are now a party of free enterprise, free trade, with free people to build a strong middle class,... here's how we're gonna do it...'" "So why didn't you do it?" Ingraham fired back. " Why didn't Biden do that? You had 4 years of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris and for part of that time you had a Democrat congress. You had all that time, Biden said it was going to be a new era for America, so what happened?!" in front of millions watching: | | POLL Are Democrats or Republicans now the party of the Middle Class? | ? ? ? ? The American working class is done getting screwed... | | ---|---|--- By Matt Miller One hundred days into President Donald J. Trump's second term, and the man is doing exactly what he said he'd do. No excuses--just results. The American people, who grind every day and sacrifice to give their families a better life, are finally seeing a White House that works for them, not the DC elites, not China or the the globalist marxists. Finally America has a President for the working class, and it's about damn time. For decades, we've been sold a lie: globalism is good, cheap Amazon crap from sweatshops is progress, and America's job is to keep up with the latest Apple iphone while our factories rust and our workers get canned. That's not an economy; that's a scam. Trump's tariffs are the economic reality check we've needed for a long time. It's not about punishing other countries; it's about giving America what it needs. His tariffs are forcing companies to think twice before shipping jobs overseas. They're bringing manufacturing back to places like Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Michigan--places the elites forgot about long ago. * PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. If we are going to protect Trump’s legacy, I MUST win. ~ | | ~ | | ~ Paid for by Rogers for Senate. | Pop Acta Media | Conservative Broadcast PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522 ~ | ~~_

name: The Squad
subject: They want to cut Medicaid by nearly $1 TRILLION
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250506-1343-foote&t=1001&akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS ]Squad Victory Fund

Hi Peter, it’s the
Squad with an urgent update from Congress:

Republicans are considering massive cuts to Medicaid, threatening to rip
health care away from MILLIONS of people. Here’s what you need to know. ⬇️

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/em20250506-em-solidarity-signon?source=em20250506-1343&akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS ]Reuters: Republicans weigh cuts to Medicaid that could dramatically
affect millions

The House and Senate – both controlled by the GOP – passed a budget that
calls for nearly $1 TRILLION in Medicaid cuts. Now, they’re combing
through, line by line, trying to figure out exactly how they’ll strip away
health care from working families to help pay for tax cuts for the rich.

While their plan isn’t finalized, the early details look disastrous: huge
cuts to services, millions losing coverage, and worse health outcomes
across the country. We need to fight back.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/em20250506-em-solidarity-signon?source=em20250506-1343&akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS ]That’s why we need you to sign our petition demanding that Republicans
keep their hands off our healthcare. Will you add your name today?

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/em20250506-em-solidarity-signon?source=em20250506-1343&akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS ]Add Your Name

We’re hard at work in the House standing up to far-right Republicans.
While they want to rip away health care from poor and disabled people just
to make the rich even richer, we want to protect working Americans and
make it easier for them to get the care they need. It’s that simple.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/em20250506-em-solidarity-signon?source=em20250506-1343&akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS ]So please, add your name to our petition demanding that Republicans
make NO cuts to Medicaid. With people across the country struggling to
afford life-saving medication, doctor visits, and more, this is the last
thing our families and communities need.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/em20250506-em-solidarity-signon?source=em20250506-1343&akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS ]Contribute ››

Thank you so much for your support. Adding your name only takes a few
seconds, but it helps show the power of our grassroots movement.

In solidarity,

The Squad

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1343%2E342411%2EgMYIQS

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

body:
Regardless of who's in office, they have a responsibility to do right by the American people. But Trump doesn't care.
Peter,

Did you catch Gov. Andy Beshear on _Fox News?_ **He didn’t hold back.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107212989/543958457/739826862?refcode=em25_250506_foxnew_fndd_gq_act___ITTPAC_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250506_foxnew_fndd_gq_act___ITTPAC_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=8893c11b-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODg5M2MxMWItYzIyYS1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=BkOAE-lKki9LcpdvVhsQm-OaA5sF0tEusAR8ySQVgjw=) Andy told the truth: Donald Trump made promises to the American people to lower costs, _but his reckless trade war is breaking that promise and sending prices skyrocketing._

**And it’s hitting Kentuckians especially hard.**

From family farms struggling to sell their crops, to small businesses paying more for materials, to working families watching prices jump even more at the grocery store — these tariffs aren’t some abstract policy debate. They’re hurting real people in communities in Kentucky and across the country.

Andy made it clear: It doesn’t matter that he’s a Democratic governor and Trump is a Republican president. When economic policies are threatening a recession and hurting the American people, Andy will speak out and oppose them, regardless of who's in office. That’s the kind of leadership we need more of, and that's why In This Together PAC is working overtime to elect common-sense, for the people leaders at EVERY level.

[**Chip in now to help us fight to put people over politics, and elect more leaders who aren’t afraid to tell the truth — no matter who’s in the White House. He didn’t hold back. = Andy told the truth: Donald Trump made promises to the American people to lower costs, but his reckless trade war is breaking that promise and sending prices skyrocketing. And it’s hitting Kentuckians especially hard. From family farms struggling to sell their crops, to small businesses paying more for materials, to working families watching prices jump even more at the grocery store — these tariffs aren’t some abstract policy debate. They’re hurting real people in communities in Kentucky and across the country. Andy made it clear: It doesn’t matter that he’s a Democratic governor and Trump is a Republican president. When economic policies are threatening a recession and hurting the American people, Andy will speak out and oppose them, regardless of who's in office. That’s the kind of leadership we need more of, and that's why In This Together PAC is working overtime to elect common-sense, for the people leaders at EVERY level. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Team In This Together PAC | Andy and In This Together PAC are working to support leaders who will lead with compassion and decency, reject anger politics, and always do what's right. Will you donate today to support In This Together's mission? = Paid for by In This Together PAC. InThisTogetherPAC.com. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to In This Together PAC are not tax deductible. In This Together PO Box 5822 Louisville, KY 40255 United States

name: Bonnie Watson Coleman
subject: ICE must be held accountable
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bonnie Watson Coleman for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107213024/543958637/1328194270?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250506_PT_ICE_0_6MO_BonnieWatsonColeman_NA_NA_ICEVisibility1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMGI5YzU2Yy1jMjJhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6OKWeRuerYEHD2dg3OCtjBs7x7ex_qD3oZ6T8-08DxM=&emci=bcb9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=c0b9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=30096098) | In the last few months, we’ve witnessed a disturbing escalation of unconstitutional tactics: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents operating in plain clothes, abducting people off the street, and instilling fear in our communities. These kinds of actions — supported and encouraged by our administration — undermine critical foundations of our democracy: due process, transparency, and the rule of law.

That’s why I just introduced the ICE Visibility Act of 2025: a bill that would require all ICE agents and officers to wear clearly marked uniforms during enforcement actions. **No more hiding behind anonymity. No more fear in our communities.

_[If you agree with Bonnie, click here to sign our petition supporting the return of due process and transparency in our institutions. We need 500 signatures by the end of the week to make a change.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107213025/543958638/-793284918?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250506_PT_ICE_0_6MO_BonnieWatsonColeman_NA_NA_ICEVisibility1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMGI5YzU2Yy1jMjJhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6OKWeRuerYEHD2dg3OCtjBs7x7ex_qD3oZ6T8-08DxM=&emci=bcb9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=c0b9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=30096098)_**_[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107213026/543958639/-793284918?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250506_PT_ICE_0_6MO_BonnieWatsonColeman_NA_NA_ICEVisibility1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMGI5YzU2Yy1jMjJhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6OKWeRuerYEHD2dg3OCtjBs7x7ex_qD3oZ6T8-08DxM=&emci=bcb9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=c0b9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=30096098)_ | | [SIGN THE PETITION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107213027/543958640/-793284918?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250506_PT_ICE_0_6MO_BonnieWatsonColeman_NA_NA_ICEVisibility1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMGI5YzU2Yy1jMjJhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6OKWeRuerYEHD2dg3OCtjBs7x7ex_qD3oZ6T8-08DxM=&emci=bcb9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=c0b9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=30096098)
---
When the Trump Administration defies court orders and uses federal agents to bypass constitutional protections, it isn’t just attacking immigrants — it’s attacking democracy itself.

ICE officers deliberately concealing their identities creates a dangerous confusion and opens the door for impersonators to intimidate individuals simply going about their day. The current administration is using ICE as a weapon of fear, creating hostile situations not only for the community but the agents as well.

The bill I have introduced is a direct response to that lawlessness. We must restore checks and balances, protect civil liberties, and make sure our communities are safe from this intimidation.

**_[Stand with us today and sign the petition to keep our community safe.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107213028/543958641/-793284918?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250506_PT_ICE_0_6MO_BonnieWatsonColeman_NA_NA_ICEVisibility1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMGI5YzU2Yy1jMjJhLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6OKWeRuerYEHD2dg3OCtjBs7x7ex_qD3oZ6T8-08DxM=&emci=bcb9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=c0b9c56c-c22a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=30096098)_**

Thank you,

Bonnie
* * *

---
Bonnie Watson Coleman represents New Jersey's 12th district in Congress. She is a strong fighter for progressive values and has dedicated herself to the service of her community.

subject: They’re attacking ActBlue. Here’s how we respond.

body:
Trump just attacked ActBlue – the tool powering our movement.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250506-1&t=1&akid=2090%2E587540%2EX7v0rM)
Peter, Donald Trump is terrified of our generation.
It couldn’t be more obvious. Trump’s terrified of our power, our momentum, and the fact that young, grassroots-backed candidates are stepping up to replace the corrupt, complacent politicians on both sides of the aisle who’ve let him run wild.
Last week, after launching a political witch hunt against ActBlue, he tweeted, slandering the trusted Democratic fundraising platform as an _**“ILLEGAL SCAM.”**_
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd_em_20250506_1_actblue_followup?refcode=em20250506-2090&:amount=5&refcode2=2090_587540_X7v0rM&akid=2090%2E587540%2EX7v0rM)
It’s worth repeating again and again: _ActBlue is still the best tool you can use to support our work._ **Contributions through ActBlue are safe, legal, and secure.**
Trump is not just going after a website. He is targeting his political opponents, aiming to tear down the infrastructure that fuels young, diverse, and progressive campaigns across the country. He’s trying to take away the power of people coming together to compete against right-wing billionaires and special interests.
**He’s trying to silence our movements. To silence _you._ And we won’t let him.**
So if you’re fired up like I am, here’s the best way to respond to Trump’s attacks:

clean_body: Trump just attacked ActBlue – the tool powering our movement. Peter, Donald Trump is terrified of our generation. It couldn’t be more obvious. Trump’s terrified of our power, our momentum, and the fact that young, grassroots-backed candidates are stepping up to replace the corrupt, complacent politicians on both sides of the aisle who’ve let him run wild. Last week, after launching a political witch hunt against ActBlue, he tweeted, slandering the trusted Democratic fundraising platform as an “ILLEGAL SCAM.” It’s worth repeating again and again: ActBlue is still the best tool you can use to support our work. Contributions through ActBlue are safe, legal, and secure. Trump is not just going after a website. He is targeting his political opponents, aiming to tear down the infrastructure that fuels young, diverse, and progressive campaigns across the country. He’s trying to take away the power of people coming together to compete against right-wing billionaires and special interests. He’s trying to silence our movements. To silence you. And we won’t let him. So if you’re fired up like I am, here’s the best way to respond to Trump’s attacks: Thank you for standing with us. In solidarity, David Hogg Leaders We Deserve

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: I'm running for re-election.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
I'm running for re-election to the U.S. Senate in Colorado
| | | Hey folks, I'm running for re-election to the U.S. Senate, and I hope you'll join me in the fight to give Colorado's priorities and values a voice in Washington.

**[Let me tell you more about this decision, then will you split a donation between my campaign and the DSCC to help build this campaign from the ground up and take back the Senate majority?](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)**

| | [Donate Now →](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)
---

Before diving into the world of politics, I took a chance, like so many small business owners do, and opened Denver's very first brewpub: Wynkoop Brewing Company. At Wynkoop, we were known for our unique beers like the Timberland Pale and Sagebrush Stout, which we offered at just $2.25 per pint – _a great deal compared to mainstream options!_

**Wynkoop was more than just a brewpub for me. It was a place where people with diverse viewpoints could come together, united by a shared love of good beer. It was a community where we proved time and again that there's hardly anything that can't be sorted out over a refreshing pint.**

Below are some nostalgic photos from the Wynkoop's early days!

[](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)
[](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)
I brought that same commitment to collaboration and problem-solving to public service—first as Mayor of Denver, then as Governor, and now as your U.S. Senator.

**And together, we've gotten things done:**

* Lowering prescription drug costs and capping insulin prices
* Investing in clean energy and fighting climate change
* Defending reproductive rights against far-right attacks
* Boosting jobs and small businesses to keep Colorado's economy strong

**But there's still so much more to do—and the stakes couldn't be higher. Trump and National Republicans are tearing apart our progress, and corporate special interests will spend millions to try and take this seat back.**

**[That's why I need you in this fight. Will you split a donation now of $5—about the cost of two Wynkoop pints—to help us build a winning campaign and flip the Senate blue?](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=9&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)**

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and be split between Hickenlooper for Colorado and the DSCC.

| [DONATE $5 →](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=10&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)
---
| [DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT →](https://go.dscc.org/go/21652?t=1&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)
---

I know we can win this the Colorado way—by bringing people together and solving problems.

Thanks for being part of the team.

Giddy up,

John

John Hickenlooper
U.S. Senator, Colorado

John Hickenlooper is running for re-election in Colorado to expand health care access, fight climate change, and so much more.
_**[Chip in now to help keep John in the Senate and fight for a Democratic Senate majority>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=11&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)**_

| | [Donate](https://go.dscc.org/go/21638?t=12&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&code=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FM-20250506-JohnHickenlooper-HickBrewpubReelectionTandem-Pros-sl1&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)
---
| |
* * *
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate, and our work relies on grassroots supporters like you. If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please **[click here](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-senate-democrats-2025-q2?refcode=DPE-footer&t=2&refcode2=20216_9716520_2bB-p5&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)**.

**[Click here](https://go.dscc.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)** to receive fewer emails. **[Click here](https://go.dscc.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=20216%2E9716520%2E2bB-p5)** to unsubscribe.

Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc.org](https://www.dscc.org/)** | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: Nellie Pou HQ
subject: Nellie votes against the Republicans' partisan budget
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pout-header-3.png]

Team,

One of Nellie's most important responsibilities in Congress is serving on the House Homeland Security Committee. On this committee, Nellie sounded the alarm on Republicans' move to give President Trump a blank check for nearly $70 billion.

They also rejected a whole lot of commonsense amendments, including some from Nellie. They included prohibiting the Trump administration from freezing FEMA funding to states, protecting children with cancer from deportation, and requiring this lawless administration to obey Supreme Court rulings. They even said no to providing job security and protections for veterans.

They said no to all of that.

This bad legislation is just the start. Republicans also plan to slash Medicaid and pass tax cuts for the wealthy. Their end goal isn't helping folks grapple with high grocery costs, it's giving more handouts to the wealthiest.

Nellie is a strong voice for North Jersey, who will always put the needs of working families, not billionaires, first. That's why we need her in Congress. Republicans see her seat as part of their math to keep and grow their House majority, so they're coming after her. From her very first vote, they've been relentlessly attacking her and have made it no secret they plan on dumping millions of dollars in our race to defeat her.

That's why we need your help. Can you chip in a few bucks today to help defend Nellie? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=250506_np_budget_l1]

Contribute [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=250506_np_budget_b2]Thank you.

— Nellie Pou HQ
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pou_headshot_crop.jpg]

About Nellie Pou
Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=2d11e236-c42a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=2d11e236-c42a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca]
Follow Nellie
https://x.com/NelliePou [https://x.com/NelliePou] https://www.facebook.com/senatorpou/ [https://www.facebook.com/senatorpou/] https://www.instagram.com/congresswomannelliepou/ [https://www.instagram.com/congresswomannelliepou/]
Checks may also be mailed to:

name: Team Bonta
subject: A full-frontal assault on our democracy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250506&t=1&akid=1774%2E1088799%2EPzqKUa)
Not only is President Trump issuing executive orders that violate the Constitution in order to execute his far-right agenda, he’s also eroding our system of checks and balances and engaging in political retribution against his enemies. _It’s a full-frontal assault on our democracy._
**One example is his attacks on law firms that were involved in any lawsuits against him, his administration, or his allies during and after his first term in office.**
During his first 100 days, the President issued an executive order punishing these companies and their lawyers by labeling them as national security risks and blacklisting them, _unless they made a deal with him._
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250506?refcode=em20250506-1774&amount=5&refcode2=1774_1088799_PzqKUa&akid=1774%2E1088799%2EPzqKUa)
While some firms have resisted and fought back, several of the nation’s largest law firms capitulated to Trump, **agreeing to provide almost $1 BILLION in pro-bono legal services to causes the Trump administration agrees with.**
Caving to these unreasonable, unprecedented, and unconstitutional demands will only embolden Trump further and create a chilling effect among legal professionals seeking to fairly and justly represent their clients.
In response, **Rob joined other Democratic attorneys general in writing an open letter to the legal community** expressing support for the firms fighting back, and urging those who bent the knee to change course and walk away from their deals with Trump.
**[As California’s Attorney General, Rob will always defend the rule of law and stand up for our democracy. If he has your support, pitch in $5 or whatever you can today to power our grassroots campaign and keep him focused on the fights at hand →](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250506?refcode=em20250506-1774&amount=5&refcode2=1774_1088799_PzqKUa&akid=1774%2E1088799%2EPzqKUa)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250506?refcode=em20250506-1774&amount=5&refcode2=1774_1088799_PzqKUa&akid=1774%2E1088799%2EPzqKUa)**
— Team Bonta
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-wrapper?refcode=em20250506&t=3&refcode2=1774_1088799_PzqKUa&akid=1774%2E1088799%2EPzqKUa)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

clean_body: Not only is President Trump issuing executive orders that violate the Constitution in order to execute his far-right agenda, he’s also eroding our system of checks and balances and engaging in political retribution against his enemies. It’s a full-frontal assault on our democracy. One example is his attacks on law firms that were involved in any lawsuits against him, his administration, or his allies during and after his first term in office. During his first 100 days, the President issued an executive order punishing these companies and their lawyers by labeling them as national security risks and blacklisting them, unless they made a deal with him. While some firms have resisted and fought back, several of the nation’s largest law firms capitulated to Trump, agreeing to provide almost $1 BILLION in pro-bono legal services to causes the Trump administration agrees with. Caving to these unreasonable, unprecedented, and unconstitutional demands will only embolden Trump further and create a chilling effect among legal professionals seeking to fairly and justly represent their clients. In response, Rob joined other Democratic attorneys general in writing an open letter to the legal community expressing support for the firms fighting back, and urging those who bent the knee to change course and walk away from their deals with Trump. — Team Bonta

Newly elected Prime Minister of Canada, Mark Carney, visited the White House today. President Trump stood firm on moving manufacturing away from Canada, including automobiles and steel, while stating the U.S. should no longer be subsidizing Canada. I was sworn back into the Board of Visitors to the U.S. Naval Academy after being fired by Biden. Never in the history of the U.S. had members been removed from a service academy role and I thank President Trump for bringing me back. A new flurry of executive orders were signed by President Trump, including ending all federal funding on gain of function research. Democrats proudly displayed Kamala Harris at the Met Gala, not hiding their allegiance to coastal elites. Meanwhile, the Trump effect has been added to the White House website, listing the trillions of dollars of investments pouring into the U.S. from deals made by President Trump. Trish Regan is with me to unpack all the movement happening within the Trump administration, including the economy, manufacturing and the U.S. breaking free from dependence on China.

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

President Trump just released his budget proposal for next year. It’s worse than we even expected.
Once again, the President and extreme conservatives are going after vital lifelines, gutting Medicaid and SNAP by more than $1 trillion. Instead of lowering costs, this budget will drive them up and take healthcare and food assistance away from those who need it most.
We won’t stand for it. Today, I joined my fellow House Democrats in signing a discharge petition – a mechanism that would require an up-or-down vote on funding for Medicaid and SNAP. Republicans should be forced to go on the record: are they for hardworking families, or not?
We need just four House Republicans to join us and preserve these basic needs. Will you support the fight to protect Medicaid and SNAP? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

subject: The counter to drag queen story hour
body:

---
| | Fellow Conservative,Before you do anything else today, will you please take a moment to **[sponsor a gift for our “See You at the Library Day” in the next 48 hours?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=39dd861526cc4f6aa87326315fd9f8bd&_e=yxnCIitlmSiD3aMgGo3snqUu4F_Yqi_V47lJJhmZ80qZ1k66GCTSX1qUyswHj0DRXf05UKPMQvkuaix95W3QcYqZANmcIczNouimOBVCP-OknJL8LUPL8EJFVyin-7oioI2jbAOKzNhm-xVNB7O13w7B-5YFO5jSiqhOAnsstkobi9k61F3mw1fj5daOJJSE_PUjlTkzqABHL6RNxA-LQk_qshhBHnCgmXCKeJjPu0NBHlc4J1wSnnQlLVl2XULvTn-A9H12zS04N-PZjQtevW5tpCCWKvUQ5HrqyO4AEoBdRiOT0JYOq71KiztvlUPlItF7GE5hH0ghFo_3auls98KGH7rnQe_UAU6ZN0x9ASz5EHaQCoXY4UT2OYizgem_54GUV2JiwZwoa5_ydHNFZw%3D%3D)** If you haven’t heard about the “See You at the Library Day” on Fox News or Newsmax or your other favorite news sources, please let me tell you why it’s so critical.
---
Back when you were growing up, you may remember lazy Saturdays at the library with a kind but stern librarian. She would fill your mind with classics like _Pat the Bunny_ and _The Little Engine That Could_. And eventually you got old enough to roam by yourself and discover adventures like the _Three Musketeers_ , _Treasure Island_ and _Charlotte’s Web_.But today, all that has changed.The president of the American Library Association (ALA) is a self-described “Marxist lesbian.”Radical librarians call _Adventure of Huckleberry Finn_ and _To Kill a Mockingbird_ “racist.” They’ve stripped Laura Ingalls Wilder’s name from a prestigious book award because of her “problematic” _Little House_ series.And they bring men in wearing full makeup, dresses, and high heels for “Drag Queen Story Hours” where books like these are read to children of all ages... A is for Activist. An ABC board book for “families who want their kids to grow up in a space that is unapologetic about activism, environmental justice, civil rights, LGBTQ rights, and everything else that activists believe in and fight for...”

| | For decades, the ultra-liberal Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation and George Soros’ Open Society have spent millions radicalizing the ALA.And as a result, a new generation of pink-haired, activist librarians have made it their mission to fill library shelves with anti-American, LGBTQ-friendly, and explicitly sexual content. Maybe you’ve noticed all the displays at your own library celebrating Pride Month or elevating “the environment” to a type of religion.The “Teen Zone” is filled with books like: _Rick_ about a cross-dressing Grandpa, _Melissa_ filled with graphic nudity, and _Darkstalker_ featuring gory violence and domestic abuse.But here’s what’s really alarming. We can’t just sit back and say, “Well, I’ll just make sure my family doesn’t go to “Drag Queen Story Hour” and we won’t check out those inappropriate books.”That may be good for your family – for now.But meanwhile, the ALA uses our taxpayer-funded libraries to lure in millions of other children who grow up to be the very activists who are destroying our country and conservative values!**[**I hope you see why I need your help today.**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=39dd861526cc4f6aa87326315fd9f8bd&_e=yxnCIitlmSiD3aMgGo3snqUu4F_Yqi_V47lJJhmZ80qZ1k66GCTSX1qUyswHj0DRXf05UKPMQvkuaix95W3QcYqZANmcIczNouimOBVCP-OknJL8LUPL8EJFVyin-7oioI2jbAOKzNhm-xVNB7O13w7B-5YFO5jSiqhOAnsstkobi9k61F3mw1fj5daOJJSE_PUjlTkzqABHL6RNxA-LQk_qshhBHnCgmXCKeJjPu0NBHlc4J1wSnnQlLVl2XULvUR0ZmEDG5rJIqqBjbhczBfMUuoNp9U84tIVmiEB7xrSo-STCIPqFAzkaUjwsjWfL5MF9Oj1CKzI2bKxbR4WRbUvrPY_wm9AdFgePUY_Rd1wSOcAoMC1fE6A-1YAQ-JYXAjxPMBfvSErLHAPEb4XNOg%3D%3D)** My name is Kirk Cameron. Maybe you recognize me from the 1980’s family sitcom “Growing Pains” or from my work in inspirational movies like “Fireproof” and “Left Behind.”I know firsthand how dangerous it is to be indoctrinated by leftist values. Pride almost took me down as a young actor in Hollywood.But the gospel saved me. I love being an actor, but what’s far more important is being a husband, dad, grandad, and a Christian.When I saw what book publishers and libraries were doing to indoctrinate children with anti-God, anti-family, anti-America messages, I knew I had a duty to take action. So together with a small Christian book publisher aptly named **Brave Books** , I wrote “As You Grow” to teach children how to grow in wisdom through the fruit of the spirit.And then we decided to go one step further and counter those awful “Drag Queen Story Hours” with a wholesome Story Hour of our own. Our idea was to read the classic books you and I grew up with plus new wholesome **Brave Books** that captivate children with lessons in bravery, honesty, kindness, and patriotism.And boy, the ALA and radical librarians didn’t like that one bit.>> Over 50 libraries rejected our request to host our Story Hour. >> The ALA helped local libraries scheme ways to keep us out including booking all the event rooms for themselves.>> Social media blew up with attacks: I was compared to a Nazi (that’s original), **Brave Books** was accused of trying to “confuse and demoralize children,” our supporters were described as “bored moms/busybodies who need something to feel good about,” and on and on. I can’t even print most things these radicals said because it’s so vile.In the end we got two libraries to reverse course by reminding them that their censorship violated the Constitution of the United States.And thousands of parents and grandparents brought their little ones to those Story Hours where we pledged allegiance to the flag, sang “God Bless America,” and read wholesome and Godly books.It was incredible.But here’s the greatest part...From there, parents and grandparents started asking **Brave Books** how they could host Story Hours of their own.And that’s how our nationwide “See You at the Library Day” was born. Last year, committed conservatives just like you booked event rooms at 360 libraries in 45 states.On the same day, we held hundreds of Story Hours to fill children’s hearts with goodness, gratitude, and God.**I went to as many "See You at the Library" events as I could in one day to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with conservative parents, grandparents, and patriots...**...for a very simple mission – to reject the Left’s radical ideas and reclaim childhood innocence in America’s libraries.And this year, our ambitious goal is to expand “See You at the Library Day” to over 1,000 libraries reaching over 100,000 children.The exact date in August will soon be announced. And before then, what we need is you!It’s going to be expensive to give these volunteers the resources they need to hold Story Hours for 100+ children at each library – that's 100,000+ children in all. **[That’s why I hope you’ll give a sponsorship gift of $175, $350, $525 or more today.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=39dd861526cc4f6aa87326315fd9f8bd&_e=yxnCIitlmSiD3aMgGo3snqUu4F_Yqi_V47lJJhmZ80qZ1k66GCTSX1qUyswHj0DRXf05UKPMQvkuaix95W3QcYqZANmcIczNouimOBVCP-OknJL8LUPL8EJFVyin-7oioI2jbAOKzNhm-xVNB7O13w7B-5YFO5jSiqhOAnsstkobi9k61F3mw1fj5daOJJSE_PUjlTkzqABHL6RNxA-LQk_qshhBHnCgmXCKeJjPu0NBHlc4J1wSnnQlLVl2XULvZXu5i8N6jlxAj-eVnL0FhDB0QBHFntK492K97tRyRu271rm6CCazBqZpNQ2m-DYAVuxk1kzOaUEEBsAHTf6hk4w64DiZ-iUi5GaD_L2b6M6XcuPq33MKF9jDQy0JEiQxDSJlHvGcMP8eb_RkEK0NVA%3D%3D)** Your sponsorship gift will be used to send a “Story Hour Kit” to every parent, grandparent, and patriot who’s organizing a “See You at the Library Day” event. The kit includes everything they need to bring your conservative values back into our public libraries: instructions for booking their library space and running their Story Hour, small American flags for the little ones, fun stickers, wholesome books, Bible memory verse cards, and other specialty items.**Brave Books** is a small Christian company. They don’t have the millions behind them like the giant taxpayer-funded ALA.Your sponsorship gift is the only way we can fund our 1,000 Story Hours to counter the radical leftists who’ve taken control of our libraries. Knowing that, **[**please consider a sponsorship gift of $175.**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=39dd861526cc4f6aa87326315fd9f8bd&_e=yxnCIitlmSiD3aMgGo3snqUu4F_Yqi_V47lJJhmZ80qZ1k66GCTSX1qUyswHj0DRXf05UKPMQvkuaix95W3QcYqZANmcIczNouimOBVCP-OknJL8LUPL8EJFVyin-7oioI2jbAOKzNhm-xVNB7O13w7B-5YFO5jSiqhOAnsstkobi9k61F3mw1fj5daOJJSE_PUjlTkzqABHL6RNxA-LQk_qshhBHnCgmXCKeJjPu0NBHlc4J1wSnnQlLVl2XULvEfxSorBybSLgDyw_oO1fZm1M0TDpGQjY5Mu4hTm8xO5M4y87S24LDVKwhwjBVB4S1HNLhoSKy3WnJDwWfEWvUqI578h_mWCCwOGkZw8QjwEqgF5AVckt5eJCMKDIwUsOTC6mENXXd7Q5L2aLXFmdCw%3D%3D)** That’s enough to sponsor one entire “Story Hour Kit” yourself. Singlehandedly, you’ll be reaching 50 children with our wholesome, Godly, and patriotic message. **[Whatever you can give whether it’s $35, $175, or anything higher or lower please get your sponsorship gift in within the next 48 hours so we know how many libraries we can get into this August!](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=39dd861526cc4f6aa87326315fd9f8bd&_e=yxnCIitlmSiD3aMgGo3snqUu4F_Yqi_V47lJJhmZ80qZ1k66GCTSX1qUyswHj0DRXf05UKPMQvkuaix95W3QcYqZANmcIczNouimOBVCP-OknJL8LUPL8EJFVyin-7oioI2jbAOKzNhm-xVNB7O13w7B-5YFO5jSiqhOAnsstkobi9k61F3mw1fj5daOJJSE_PUjlTkzqABHL6RNxA-LQk_qshhBHnCgmXCKeJjPu0NBHlc4J1wSnnQlLVl2XULvJ3mcU1lyNJXteffKPJsUvG83qxy06AH0NJIL2sWS4uVK98Z99-BZ1qt2qs9QDDP9_iROOWyAKNarfIY1lm9_LdvJWNM4pQjgRXNLhHzEt1OgR3doLkGSk58y0zcKsJ0nfVLzESsMqMQYcPqlWHw5bg%3D%3D)**

So today, on **National Teachers’ Day**, we want to honor all of the incredible teachers out there who dedicate their lives to shaping the minds of our children. That’s why we continue to advocate for bipartisan solutions that prioritize increased funding for education and supporting workforce development. ** [**Please sign our card to show appreciation for our teachers today!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107214446/543963880/-1005246269?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk4MjcyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImY4ZGIwNjFlLWM3MmEtZjAxMS04YjNkLTYwNDViZGVkOGNjYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6H87I6SUY7zqez2uVUlF8__ifqJv2XE0PSw3HwfSXOs=&emci=f0bf4255-a52a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=f8db061e-c72a-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=15125741) Let’s keep fighting for the educators who give our kids a brighter future, \- The Blue Dogs

Blue Dogs are normal people in Congress who want to get sh*t done. We believe in freedom and we’re fighting to protect yours. We’re pro-America, pro-democracy, and working to pass fiscally responsible policies that benefit everyday, hard-working Americans. [**We need your help to defeat the extemists in D.C. _This guest column originally appeard in The Coffman Chronicle, an indispensable source of independent media, where Marie Riverton is Editor. Subscribe to[The Coffman Chronicle](https://substack.com/redirect/1cfd31e5-ea59-4b4f-aef1-13ae2f002e00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), you'll be smarter for it. And subscribe to [Marie's Substack](https://substack.com/redirect/a3015077-2530-4254-b2ac-bac264ed9ad3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
### **The Myth of the Pastoral Dream**
We are told a story about rural America.
It's the story politicians love to tell--especially the GOP--when they wrap themselves in flags and plant themselves on dirt roads. They speak of endless fields, clean air, church bells, hard work, and proud, simple lives. They call it "the real America." And they promise to protect it.
It's the one politicians--especially the ones who cut ribbons in cowboy boots and talk about God and grit--love to repeat. That life there is clean and proud, that they live off the land and care for each other. That they don't need much, and don't ask for more.
It's the story they tell while cutting school lunch programs. While closing clinics. While gutting disaster preparedness and mental health services. Letting dollar stores replace grocery stores and watching local farms die while commodity corn feeds cattle and CEOs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5d51a9c9-c3e8-4847-8cce-47e0993c5f8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
`Independent News For Just $1.50/Week!`_**
**_We recently launched our Substack show,**" Amped Up w/ Cliff Schecter."** _**To thank you for your support, we 're gifting** **you**_**an all-access pass** _**to Blue Amp for just $1.50/week ($60/yr)!**_
**G** _**et exclusive access/analysis behind the velvet rope --what corporate media **_**won 't tell you. **
_**Amplify Good. Fight Evil. Support Independent Press**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.UlvGaAMt-OjdQA8i5cc55tYbhYPr_4JiuJcLlMoKv0k?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=163006727)
The truth? Rural America is being abandoned--not passively, not by accident, but through policy, through design. The factories are gone, the grocery stores are empty, and the hospitals are closing. The kids leave, and most don't come back. And while the towns hollow out, the leaders who claim to love rural America keep cutting the very programs that once helped us survive--education, food, health care, community support, and disaster relief.
This isn't neglect. [It's betrayal wrapped in patriotism](https://substack.com/redirect/a1277b24-b074-4868-91d3-8b94140c4f44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's happening in the background and by design, under Trump 2.0 and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/02433a14-e580-44d1-9880-9c07de579c64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
They wave the flag. Then they cut the lifelines.
They speak of loyalty. Then they leave us to fend for ourselves.
What's happening to rural America isn't natural decline; it's manufactured collapse.
* * *
## **The Collapse of Opportunity**
The promise was simple in rural towns: work hard, and doors would open.
Those seeking more education face the zip code obstacle. Few small rural schools can offer college prep, AP, or advanced coursework to attract the interest of university recruiters. Unfortunately, the valedictorian of a class of 30 isn't on a level playing field with the top ten in a class of thousands.
And an athletic scholarship? Few exist, so small-town sportsmen and women must compete against better-funded programs that benefit from extensive training camps and competition, which lure lucrative recruiters and news coverage.
That leaves laborious grant and scholarship hunts, decades of loan payments, and work-study programs barely covering toiletries.
If college wasn't your path, there were still trades to learn, farms to work, and decent jobs to earn a living.
Or at least there was.
[The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)--Trump's ideological wrecking ball](https://substack.com/redirect/c0237a6f-2a7c-4892-ac02-86dddce189b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--has targeted the few programs that gave rural youth a path forward. Now, the doors are closing for good.
### **AmeriCorps: Service as a Stepping Stone --Now Defunded**
AmeriCorps wasn't just a "volunteer program." In rural areas, it was one of the only structured opportunities to gain experience, earn a stipend, and receive college tuition help without leaving your hometown.
AmeriCorps placed tens of thousands of young adults in roles like:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/628fc685-1dc4-4555-89e7-95b60d0cb3ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It helped students bridge the gap from high school to college or career, especially in places without recruiters, AP classes, or job pipelines. Best of all, the program was structured to last 12 to 24 months, rather than a long-term commitment.
In 2025, AmeriCorps lost nearly $400 million in funding under DOGE's cuts, displacing over 32,000 volunteers and gutting over 1,000 programs, including in rural hubs in West Virginia, Mississippi, Iowa, and northern Nevada.
* * *
### **Job Corps: Vocational Training for the Working Class --Now Gutted**
Job Corps was designed for exactly the kind of student rural America produces: hardworking, hands-on, not headed to a four-year university. It offered free trade training, housing, meals, and career placement in fields like welding, CNA certification, auto tech, carpentry, and IT.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d80a9b3c-e8ff-4973-9756-3c9f58ecfb7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Trump's 2025 budget proposes eliminating the program entirely as part of a 35% cut to the Department of Labor. That's not efficiency; that's erasure. Programs are designed to last months, not years.
With Job Corps centers closing, rural youth lose access to:
* Safe, structured job training
* Professional certifications
* The support network they need to get stable work and avoid poverty or enlistment by default

### **The Military Isn 't a Substitute**
When schools fail and trades disappear, recruiters show up. The military becomes the fallback.
But here's the truth:
* Not everyone can serve. Health issues, trauma histories, disabilities, and even weight disqualify many.
* Others are morally or spiritually opposed to military service.
* In an era of rising global conflict, joining the military is not a neutral career step; it's a high-stakes, high-risk gamble.
* The promise of the GI Bill requires a multi-year commitment, often far from home.
* [Upon return to their communities, veterans may face an enhanced need for specialized medical care](https://substack.com/redirect/1efa1735-f666-4a32-88bb-805084415cba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and limited job opportunities.

### **No Local Jobs, No Options, No Exit**
Once, rural towns had jobs in timber, mining, manufacturing, rail, or farming. Now?
Those have been offshored, mechanized, or consolidated. What's left are:
* Minimum wage service jobs with no benefits
* Gig work with no security
* Dangerous meatpacking or agricultural labor, often done under threat and without recourse.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8e97c757-b43d-413d-9c9a-f247ef995896?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
`Independent News For Just $1.50/Week!`_**

## **Food Deserts in a Sea of Grain**
You can stand in the middle of a rural town and see cornfields in every direction. But inside the local gas station--one of the only places left to buy food--there are no tomatoes, fresh greens, or milk that hasn't expired--just shelves of boxed noodles, chips, and soda.
That's the paradox of food in rural America: they grow it but can't eat it.
### **Fields of Grain, Not Food**
Most rural farms aren't growing food for the table. They're growing commodity crops like soybeans, wheat, and corn. But this is not the kind you eat off the cob. This is cow corn, grown for animal feed, ethanol, or processed food ingredients.
* In 2023, over 70 million acres were planted with field corn, yet less than 1% of it was edible by humans.
* These crops receive nearly $30 billion in annual subsidies, while fruits and vegetables--classified as "specialty crops"--receive less than 1% of that.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dd049712-4937-456c-9a12-bded7f076790?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Why? Because federal policy rewards bulk, not nutrition.
So even if a farmer _wanted_ to grow food for local sale, they'd face:
* No subsidies or price protections
* Higher labor and insurance costs
* A market with no local distributor or school buyer

Rural areas grow what makes money, not what feeds people. And the people get sick.
### **Grocery Stores Replaced by Dollar Chains**
In the last 15 years, rural America has lost thousands of local grocers. Dollar Generals, Family Dollars, and gas station markets have replaced them.
* 13.5 million people in the U.S. now live in food deserts, most in rural and southern counties.
* Rural counties have seen a 136% increase in dollar stores since 2008.
* These stores carry processed, packaged goods, not fresh produce, non-frozen meat, and limited dairy.

You can buy 15 kinds of snack cakes, but not a single apple.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4770ec1a-ceac-42c7-ba4c-679b603150cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **Cuts to USDA Nutrition Programs Made It Worse**
The one place rural kids might reliably get a healthy meal? School.
Programs like Farm to School and Local Food for Schools used to:
* Fund fresh food from local farms
* Provide salad bars and cooking classes
* Introduce students to real food, not reheated slop

However, in 2025, both programs were eliminated under Trump's USDA, removing over $660 million in direct food and grant support to schools and nonprofits.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9ac4cefe-aa66-4cb0-b5be-9e44eeff9830?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Result: kids eat more processed meals, local farmers lose income, and fresh produce rots in fields.
In a land of abundance, kids go hungry. Not because rural communities don't grow food, but because the system is rigged not to feed them.
* * *
## **No Clinics, No Care**
What happens when you live in a food desert? Chronic health struggles with limited access to care. In rural America, health care doesn't start late. It often doesn't start at all. For millions, the closest emergency room is over an hour away. That's not an inconvenience; it's the difference between life and death in a farm accident, a heart attack, or a car wreck on a gravel road.
This isn't because rural Americans don't care about health. The system that once cared for them is vanishing, cut, closed, or consolidated out of reach.
Worse yet, chronic health issues disproportionately impact rural communities.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f29b5a2f-5183-47ec-a12d-e5c7d3376bfd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **Rural Hospitals Are Disappearing**
The numbers are stark:
* Over 190 rural hospitals are at immediate risk of closure, and over 600 are vulnerable nationwide.
* Since 2005, more than 150 rural hospitals have closed, more than 80% in states that didn't expand Medicaid.
* The Trump 2.0 budget proposes deep cuts to Medicare and Medicaid, two lifelines for rural hospitals.

Without those reimbursements, hospitals can't survive.
What this means:
* No maternity care. No ER. No trauma unit.
* A farm injury becomes a fatality.
* Cancer goes undiagnosed until it's too late.
* Heart disease equals a death sentence.

### **Mental Health: Crisis Without Care**
As the body declines, as poverty escalates, the mind suffers. It results in addiction and mental health concerns. The mental health crisis in rural America is not theoretical. It's in the headlines, the obituaries, and the conversations we're still afraid to have.
* Suicide rates in rural counties are 46% higher than in urban areas.
* Over 60% of rural Americans live in mental health professional shortage areas.
* Farmers face one of the highest suicide rates by profession in the U.S.

When AmeriCorps was funded, it helped:
* Staff school counseling programs
* Support community mental health outreach
* Provide peer mentorship and wellness checks

Those services are now gone in many towns. And with stigma still high, private therapy often doesn't fill the gap.
We tell young men to tough it out. But tough doesn't get you a therapist.

### **Telehealth Is Not a Fix Without Infrastructure**
Politicians pitch telehealth like it's the answer to rural healthcare collapse. But what good is a Zoom appointment when:
* 1 in 3 rural households lack broadband?
* Your cell signal dies halfway up the driveway?
* Your grandparents don't own a laptop, much less know how to use one?

Telehealth can be powerful only if you have power, signal, and access.
You can't treat a heart attack with a hotspot.
### **DOGE and Trump 2.0 Are Making It Worse**
The Department of Government Efficiency isn't just cutting services. It's erasing the agencies that rural communities rely on:
* Health navigators: gone.
* Rural wellness grants: slashed.
* School-based health programs: dismantled.
* AmeriCorps public health placements: eliminated.

A ghost system remains--empty buildings, burned-out providers, and no one left to care.
We didn't lose healthcare. It was taken from us, program by program, dollar by dollar, life by life.
* * *
## **Disaster Capitalism Replacing Disaster Relief**
Even if rural residents weather the economic crisis, lack of affordable nutrition, and the absence of healthcare, no one can outrun Mother Nature. When disaster hits a rural town--a flood, a wildfire, a tornado--there's little national media coverage. No FEMA tent cities on cable news. Just neighbors trying to drag each other out of the mud.
The infrastructure that once helped rural communities prepare, respond, and rebuild is being dismantled, [not just through budget neglect, but also through active policy](https://substack.com/redirect/0d06e34c-7c00-4b5c-a90c-0ee8fb7ceaf3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **AmeriCorps: Cut Before the Storm**
Before FEMA ever shows up, AmeriCorps teams were often already on the ground:
* Training communities for emergency preparedness
* Helping with flood prevention, tree clearing, and evacuation planning
* Organizing shelters and emergency supply distribution

In many rural areas, these teams were the first line of defense. But in 2025, AmeriCorps was gutted, displacing tens of thousands of volunteers and wiping out entire community resilience programs.
AmeriCorps helped rural areas prepare for the worst. Now they face it alone.
### **FEMA: Slower, Smaller, Underfunded**
FEMA is supposed to respond when disaster hits, but under Trump 2.0, it operates with fewer resources and less authority, especially for equity-focused programs that benefit rural communities.
* Grants for climate resilience and mitigation? Cut.
* Programs for underserved regions? Scrapped.
* FEMA's workforce? Shrinking.

This means that when the levee breaks or the wildfire hits, help may take days, or never come at all.
They said help was coming. But by then, the flood had taken everything.
North Carolina, Georgia, and Tennessee are still trying to recover from Hurricane Helene six months ago. And the Trump administration's FEMA denied aid for tornado recovery in Arkansas, flood remediation in West Virginia, and windstorm damage in Washington in late April.
### **USDA "Recovery" = Infrastructure, Not People**
Yes, USDA has announced rural disaster aid, but that money doesn't rebuild lives. It rebuilds:
* Water systems
* Power lines
* Business infrastructure

It doesn't fund:
* Housing for displaced families
* Trauma care or community counseling
* Emergency nutrition or long-term shelter

They'll fix the grain elevator. But not the house your kids used to sleep in.
### **The Pattern: Preparedness Cut. Response Delayed. Recovery Privatized.**
This isn't a broken system. It's a new one, a model that:
1. Cuts the programs that prepared us (AmeriCorps).
2. Defunds the agencies that respond to us (FEMA).
3. Prioritizes rebuilding what makes money (USDA disaster loans and contracts), not what makes a community whole.

This is disaster capitalism in action: profit-driven recovery where people come last.
Rural areas are not being rescued. They're being written off. Disaster response has become a profit model, and rural America is the loss they're willing to absorb.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/639dcba7-2bc7-4c59-84a2-a0293f6b4ede?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
## **The Collapse of Community**
Politicians love to talk about how small communities look out for one another. And in most cases, they do. But communities are built around infrastructure, around buildings, and ideals. Before the clinics closed and the stores left, there were also places where people could gather, learn, and get help: the library, the food pantry, the nonprofit next door. These weren't luxuries. They were the last functioning infrastructure of rural life.
Now, they're being quietly erased, cut down by the same Trump 2.0 policies and DOGE budget ax that's cleared out everything else.
### **Libraries: The Last Public Space, Now On the Chopping Block**
In rural towns, the library does everything:
* Provides free internet where broadband doesn't exist
* Hosts reading hours, job search workshops, and tax prep help
* Distributes food in the summer when school meals disappear

However, under the Trump 2025 budget, the Institute of Museum and Library Services (IMLS)--the only federal agency that funds libraries--is targeted for elimination.
That means rural libraries lose:
* Bookmobiles serving the housebound and care facilities
* After-school programming providing a safe place while parents work
* STEM learning kits to enhance struggling schools
* Emergency broadband access, computers, and tech help

First, the local clinic. Then the grocery store closed. Now the administration wants to take the library? What do they want rural people to do--sit in the dark and wait to die?
### **Nonprofits: Filling the Gaps, Now Strangled by Cuts**
Nonprofits have been the duct tape holding rural America together:
* Delivering meals to the elderly and disabled
* Helping foster families and elders
* Running warming shelters in the winter and cooling centers in the heat

However, with federal funding frozen or slashed under DOGE, entire organizations are shutting down. Many relied on:
* AmeriCorps volunteers
* USDA community grants
* Matching federal dollars to access state funds

Now those resources are gone. And in towns with no city hall and budget, no one is left to pick up the slack. And the need? It keeps growing.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4fd3394c-0f85-4518-a73d-b6506f934bc4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **Schools: Still Open, But Starving**
Rural schools are still running--barely. But they've lost:
* Fresh food programs
* Behavioral health supports
* After-school enrichment and summer learning

What's left are teachers who buy snacks and supplies with their own paychecks, and students who come to class hungry, anxious, and underserved.
What happens when the only infrastructure keeping families going is stripped away? If privatization is the goal, who do they expect to pay for it?
They say it takes a village, but the village is gone, and now they're taking the last few people standing in the square.
* * *
## **Feudalism in Capitalist Clothing**
This isn't just economic hardship. It's neo-feudalism in a red hat:
* Labor with no reward.
* Loyalty with no protection.
* Lives sacrificed so corporations can flourish and billionaires can fundraise from the wreckage.

They don't want rural America to die. They want it to kneel.
This isn't a government of the people. It's a castle on a hill, watching the villages burn.
Rural America is not broken because its people are lazy or backward. It's being broken on purpose.
While politicians sell slogans and symbols, they're stripping away the programs that sustain rural life, not out of ignorance, but out of design.
This isn't conservative governance. It's exploitation.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ad333f99-5cf3-48f4-af4f-b677610787b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
We owe it to rural America not to mythologize it, but to fight for it. To name the betrayal. To resist the lies. And to demand a future where dignity, health, and hope aren't reserved for city skylines or gated communities, but rooted in every dirt road, every small school, and every farm struggling to survive.
There is nothing weak about wanting your child to eat real food, your parents to see a doctor, and your neighbor to survive a flood. There is nothing un-American about demanding that the systems we fund with our taxes work for us, not just for billionaires and lobbyists.
This isn't about left or right. It's about survival. It's about truth.
If rural America is dying, it's not from lack of faith, work ethic, or pride. It's from a thousand cuts, inflicted by the very hands that claimed to protect it.
We can't afford to keep swallowing the lie. Because the collapse isn't coming. It's already here.

Subscribe to, you'll be smarter for it. And subscribe to . The Myth of the Pastoral Dream We are told a story about rural America. It's the story politicians love to tell--especially the GOP--when they wrap themselves in flags and plant themselves on dirt roads. They speak of endless fields, clean air, church bells, hard work, and proud, simple lives. They call it "the real America." And they promise to protect it. It's the one politicians--especially the ones who cut ribbons in cowboy boots and talk about God and grit--love to repeat. That life there is clean and proud, that they live off the land and care for each other. That they don't need much, and don't ask for more. It's the story they tell while cutting school lunch programs. While closing clinics. While gutting disaster preparedness and mental health services. Letting dollar stores replace grocery stores and watching local farms die while commodity corn feeds cattle and CEOs. | | ---|---|--- `Independent News For Just $1.50/Week!` We recently launched our Substack show," Amped Up w/ Cliff Schecter."
